title: basilima pygmaea
intro: Basilima pygmaea, also known as Pygmy Basil, is a small, herbaceous perennial plant native to Southeast Asia, belonging to the family Lamiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Basilima pygmaea include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and provide antioxidant properties, which can help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. Basilima pygmaea is often prepared as an herbal infusion or decoction, and its leaves can also be used fresh in cooking or dried for later use.
